title: enable netavark specific tests
body:
Signed-off-by: Lokesh Mandvekar <lsm5@fedoraproject.org>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *lsm5* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [lsm5] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
should be a much lighter PR once c/common changes are vendored.

@flouthoc PTAL at the current version, it should run 5 tests, 2 pass, 3 failures. I have mentioned the passing tests in the git log. This occurs with the latest version of netavark and aardvark from `rhcontainerbot/podman-next` copr.
btw, you might need to run it in permissive mode, I was getting many conmon permission denied errors otherwise.

@lsm5 @baude All the tests should pass with latest `aardvark` on CI. I verified it manually on my machine.

However when you are running tests on you local most likely your pod `pause` image is going to buggy cause with `podman4.0` we build `pause` locally and if you switch to latest podman and using older `catatonit` it causes podman to produce a buggy `pause` image.

So when running on local either get a fresh pull of `catatonit` and remove all existing pause image or specify a custom `pause` image like.  Also this `pause` image issue is network agnostic and exists for `CNI` as well if `catatonit` on your system was old.

```diff
diff --git a/test/e2e/run_networking_test.go b/test/e2e/run_networking_test.go
index f6604c216..c85203252 100644
--- a/test/e2e/run_networking_test.go
+++ b/test/e2e/run_networking_test.go
@@ -878,7 +878,7 @@ EXPOSE 2004-2005/tcp`, ALPINE)
        FIt("podman run check dnsname plugin with Netavark", func() {
                SkipIfCNI(podmanTest)
                pod := "testpod"
-               session := podmanTest.Podman([]string{"pod", "create", "--name", pod})
+               session := podmanTest.Podman([]string{"pod", "create", "--name", pod, "--infra-image", "k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2"})
                session.WaitWithDefaultTimeout()
                Expect(session).Should(Exit(0))
 
@@ -889,7 +889,7 @@ EXPOSE 2004-2005/tcp`, ALPINE)
                Expect(session).Should(Exit(0))
 
                pod2 := "testpod2"
-               session = podmanTest.Podman([]string{"pod", "create", "--network", net, "--name", pod2})
+               session = podmanTest.Podman([]string{"pod", "create", "--network", net, "--name", pod2, "--infra-image", "k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2"})
                session.WaitWithDefaultTimeout()
                Expect(session).Should(Exit(0))
```
All 5 tests pass locally, the issues noticed earlier today were because of an older container-selinux on my system. Marked this as ready for review.
